sections.heading: Why Performance Matters
sections.content: App performance affects user retention, ratings, and revenue. Android targets 60fps (16ms per frame); delays cause jank. Performance includes startup time, memory usage, battery, network efficiency. Measure first using Android Studio Profilers (CPU, Memory, Network, Energy) before optimizing.

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Keep main thread free — use coroutines: Dispatchers.IO for I/O, Dispatchers.Default for CPU work
Use StrictMode in debug to catch violations early
Clear View references in onDestroyView(), cancel coroutines appropriately
Use LeakCanary for automatic memory leak detection
Optimize layouts: flatten hierarchies, use ConstraintLayout, ViewStub, merge
RecyclerView: DiffUtil, stable IDs, prefetch, avoid object allocation in onBindViewHolder
Battery: WorkManager, batch requests, release wakelocks, avoid frequent alarms
Use Baseline Profiles and Macrobenchmark to reduce startup and JIT overhead

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Profile first, then optimize. Keep main thread responsive, prevent memory leaks, defer non-critical initialization, flatten layouts, and adopt Baseline Profiles. Use appropriate threading, LeakCanary, WorkManager, and Macrobenchmark to maintain smooth, efficient, and battery-friendly apps.
